prompt: I just got a pay rise to £112,000 and my accountant friend mentioned something about losing my personal allowance which means I'm effectively paying 60% tax on some of my income?? That sounds mad. I also get child benefit for our 3 kids. I've got a workplace pension where my employer puts in 5% and I put in 5% through salary sacrifice. What can I do to not get absolutely hammered? Should I put more in my pension? My wife earns about £28,000.

assertions.text: Explains 60% effective rate between £100,000 and £125,140 due to PA tapering
Calculates current PA loss (£112,000 - £100,000 = £12,000; PA reduced by £6,000)
Recommends increasing salary sacrifice pension to bring ANI below £100,000
Calculates the specific additional pension contribution needed to get below £100k
Explains NI savings benefit of salary sacrifice vs personal pension contributions
Mentions marriage allowance (wife at £28,000 is basic rate, eligible to transfer)
Provides quantified total tax savings from recommended strategies
